sourceMovie.title: The Night of the Hunter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Night of the Hunter (1955) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Payback
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Night of the Hunter clicked for you, Payback (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Night of the Hunter?
faq.answer: Try Payback, Fallen, The Informer—each shares DNA with The Night of the Hunter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Night of the Hunter?
faq.answer: The Night of the Hunter (1955) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Night of the Hunter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
